the country's constitution has been

African country have been going on for

Omar al-bashir seized power in Sudan
through a military coup in 1989 he went
on to dissolve the government all
political parties and trade unions his
harsh rule over his people eventually
amounted to the International Criminal
Court issuing an arrest warrant for the
Sudanese leader Bashir is wanted for
crimes against humanity war crimes and
genocide for atrocities allegedly
committed in Sudan's Western Darfur
region Bashir is accused of enlisting a
brutal Arab militia called the Janjaweed
to quell and raced against his rule in
the region he denied the ICC's
allegations and disputed reports the
tens of thousands of people were killed
or displaced by the fighting the first
thing is that we don't agree with these

despite this countries such as Egypt
South Africa and Saudi Arabia still
allowed him to make diplomatic visits in
the bloody decades long civil war waged

South Sudan's secession in 2011 the two
countries continued to fight the in
cross-border skirmishes for control of
the country's disputed and lucrative oil

Bashir continued to win elections in his

but any signs of internal dissent such
